MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Paul Brumett

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE W.W. HENRY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The W.W. Henry Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant's separate statement fails to lay out undisputed facts demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Henry's mastic to which he was exposed contained asbestos. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Plaintiff's objections to Lori Angelo's deposition testimony are sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Counsel for defendant requested to incorporate the parties' argument in #275898 Lee v. Thomas Dee Engineering into the record. The Court granted the request and adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to submit evidence negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J.
